title: Find Case-Insensitive Palindrome Words

wrong_answer_rejection.details: Empty=0.353, Partial=0.588, Case-sensitive=0.588. All below 0.6 pass_threshold. Partial and case-sensitive landing at exactly 0.588 is a tight margin — a reviewer might want to bump the weight of line_count or the file_match anchors slightly to widen the gap.

anti_gaming.details: Hardcoded palindromes.txt with level/racecar/Civic/Kayak/XXX scores 1.0. Missing: file_match regex='^noon$' — noon is the 5th true palindrome and leaving it un-pinned allows any filler on line 5. One added check closes this specific hole.

style_variation.details: Rewrite using regex back-reference fullmatch for palindrome detection, open()+read()+splitlines(), writelines() generator, imperative loops with .append — scored 1.0.

hardening_required: Add file_match regex='^noon$' flags='m' weight=2 — pins the 5th expected palindrome
(Optional) Add file_match regex='^noon\n' to verify the line terminator (trailing-newline spec)
suggestions: Consider adding an Arabic or accented word to input that would be FILTERED OUT, and a file_not_match to verify it's absent — tests case-handling behavior more thoroughly.

Non-triviality score is 1 because solution is ~6 lines. A medium-difficulty palindrome task could require handling punctuation, multi-word phrases, or case preservation with Unicode — consider as follow-up task.
meta_note: Same Test B meta-note as mb-001. After the one-line hardening (add ^noon$), the hardcode attack requires knowing 5 exact strings, which is just the nature of output checks.
reasoning: Clear spec, clean solution, checks reject most wrong answers. The 5th-line gap is a clean fix. Recommend ACCEPT with the single ^noon$ addition.
